title: Bulk invoice CSV upload fails with 500 error for enterprise customers
description: Enterprise customers uploading CSV files with more than 500 invoice rows receive HTTP 500 Internal Server Error. The payment-service Node.js process crashes with 'JavaScript heap out of memory' when parsing large CSVs synchronously. Impacting GlobalTech Corp's finance team operations. Log snippet: ERR_HEAP_OUT_OF_MEMORY allocation failed - process out of memory. Stack trace points to csv-parse sync parser in /services/upload/validate.js:142.
stepsToReproduce: Upload a CSV file with 1000+ invoice rows via the enterprise upload portal
Observe 500 error returned after 30-45 seconds
Check payment-service logs for heap allocation failure
Note that files under 200 rows process successfully

title: Auth service latency spike during peak hours
description: Overnight monitoring alert: auth service P95 latency spiked to 12 seconds between 22:00-23:00 UTC. Database CPU at 98%. Initial analysis suggests connection pool exhaustion from a runaway batch job. Incident created automatically by Datadog monitor. No customer-facing impact reported yet but trending toward SLO breach. Logs show 500+ concurrent connections when pool max is 100.
stepsToReproduce: Check Datadog dashboard for auth-service latency metrics
Review RDS CPU utilization for the 22:00-23:00 window
Identify the batch job that triggered connection storm

title: Payment retry handler exhausting database connection pool
description: The payment retry handler creates new database connections for each retry attempt instead of using the connection pool. During the GlobalTech bulk upload incident, 500+ failed payment attempts created cascading connection exhaustion, taking down other payment processing. Fix requires adding a semaphore or circuit breaker to the retry mechanism.
stepsToReproduce: Simulate 200+ concurrent payment failures
Monitor DB connection count — observe unbounded growth
Notice other payment endpoints timing out due to pool exhaustion

title: Invoice API returns wrong due date for Western hemisphere customers
description: Invoice API returns due dates that are one day early for customers in UTC-5 to UTC-8 timezones. The date calculation uses UTC midnight but does not offset for customer timezone. Customers see invoices as 'due today' when they have one more day. Fix confirmed by Priya — pending Alex's code review sign-off before deploying to production.
stepsToReproduce: Set customer timezone to America/New_York (UTC-5)
Create invoice with due date 2024-01-15
API returns due date as 2024-01-14
resolutionNotes: Fix ready in PR #891. Waiting for Alex Chen to review and approve.

title: Dashboard reporting page times out for accounts with 50k+ invoices
description: The dashboard reporting page takes over 10 seconds to load for enterprise accounts with more than 50,000 invoices. The invoice list query performs a full table scan instead of using indexed pagination. Fix is dependent on Alex's bulk upload refactor (PHOE-1198) due to shared database query paths.
stepsToReproduce: Log in as enterprise admin with 50k+ invoices
Navigate to reporting dashboard
Observe >10s load time
resolutionNotes: Blocked on PHOE-1198. Marcus has the fix prepared locally.

title: Email notification for payment confirmation renders broken tables
description: Payment confirmation email notifications render with broken table layouts when viewed in Outlook and Gmail HTML email clients. The responsive template uses CSS properties not supported by these email clients (flexbox, grid). Plain text fallback works but appears unprofessional for enterprise customers.
stepsToReproduce: Trigger a payment confirmation notification
Open the email in Outlook desktop client
Observe misaligned table cells and broken layout

title: Draft invoices not cleaned up after 30 days
description: Draft invoice records that are never finalized are not being cleaned up by the scheduled cleanup job. Over 12,000 draft records accumulated over 6 months, adding ~500ms to invoice list queries. The cleanup cron job exists but has a bug in the date filter — it checks 'updated_at' instead of 'created_at', missing old drafts that were never touched.
stepsToReproduce: Check drafts table — count records older than 30 days
Verify cleanup job SQL query filters on updated_at instead of created_at
Run corrected query to confirm drafts would be cleaned

title: Webhook signature verification fails intermittently
description: Webhook signature verification fails approximately 2% of the time for enterprise integrations. The issue is a race condition in the nonce tracking — when two webhook events arrive simultaneously, the nonce check fails for the second event because the cache hasn't been invalidated. This causes legitimate webhook retries to be rejected.
stepsToReproduce: Send two identical webhook events within 100ms
First event succeeds, second is rejected with 'invalid signature'
Check nonce cache — second event's nonce incorrectly marked as used

title: Login page footer overlaps with content on mobile Safari
description: On mobile Safari (iOS 17), the login page footer overlaps with the login form content when the keyboard is open. This is a CSS rendering issue with the sticky footer position not accounting for the Safari viewport height change when the keyboard appears.
stepsToReproduce: Open login page on iPhone Safari (iOS 17+)
Tap on email input field to open keyboard
Observe footer overlapping with login button
